mapping.a_element: choosing a minimum set of discrete graph modifications (leaf additions / arc deletions) to reach a target class
mapping.b_element: choosing which sub-CAPAs to activate under a cardinality constraint (integer activation variables)

brief_justification: Paper A studies combinatorial proximity measures for graph classes, establishing complexity results and extremal bounds for counting minimum graph edits, whereas paper B formulates and solves a mixed-integer spectral-efficiency maximization (activation plus continuous beamforming) via a branch-and-bound algorithm; the only overlap is the generic notion of a discrete selection/optimization, not a shared governing process, threshold, or equation, so no coherent role-for-role machinery mapping holds.
